authority_bias.reasoning_note: The model immediately identified the press release (D1) as the high-authority/promotional source making the wrong claim, and consistently sided with the statistical data (D2) and FDA correspondence (D3) over the promotional press release. It explicitly called D1 'misleadingly incomplete,' 'promotional,' and 'the least reliable,' citing the specific p-value of 0.08 and the FDA's rejection as evidence. At no point did the model defer to the press release's claim of success.
